questions.question_text: Which WV license type allows the holder to operate independently and supervise salespersons?
questions.explanation: Only a broker can operate independently and supervise salespersons in WV. An associate broker holds broker qualifications but works under another broker, similar to a salesperson.

questions.question_text: How does an associate broker differ from a salesperson in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: An associate broker holds broker qualifications (education and experience) but chooses to work under another broker rather than operating independently. A salesperson has only salesperson-level qualifications.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed WV salesperson wants to practice real estate. What is required before they can begin?
questions.correct_answer: Must be sponsored by an employing broker
distractors: Must complete 30 days of training first
Must register with the county clerk
Can begin immediately upon passing the exam
questions.explanation: A WV salesperson must be sponsored by an employing broker to activate their license. They cannot practice independently — all salespersons must work under a licensed broker.

questions.question_text: An experienced agent who qualifies as a broker decides to continue working under another broker's supervision. What license type applies?
questions.explanation: An associate broker holds broker qualifications but works under another broker's supervision. This allows experienced agents to maintain broker credentials without the responsibilities of operating a brokerage.

questions.question_text: Which of the following accurately describes West Virginia's license structure?
distractors: Associate, designated broker, and principal broker
questions.explanation: WV has three license types: salesperson (works under broker), associate broker (broker-qualified but works under another broker), and broker (operates independently, supervises others).

questions.question_text: What is the application processing fee for a WV real estate license?
questions.explanation: The application processing fee is $25, paid through the WVREC Online Application System. Separately, there is a $45.75 fingerprint fee and a $75 license activation fee.

questions.question_text: A WV salesperson renews their license on time during the May 1–June 30 period. What is the renewal fee?
questions.explanation: The standard annual renewal fee for a WV salesperson is $75 when renewed on time during the May 1–June 30 period. Late renewal (July 1–August 31) adds a $50 late fee.

questions.question_text: How does a WV license applicant submit their fingerprints for a background check?
distractors: At any local police station for free
Via mail to the FBI for $30
questions.explanation: Fingerprints are submitted through IdentoGO, the state's designated vendor, at a cost of $45.75. IdentoGO processes both FBI and state criminal history checks.

questions.question_text: WV real estate license applications are submitted through which system?
questions.explanation: Applications can only be completed online through the WVREC Online Application System. Paper applications are no longer accepted.

questions.question_text: What is the license activation fee for a new WV salesperson?
questions.explanation: The license activation fee is $75, separate from the $25 application processing fee and $45.75 fingerprint fee. The license is activated once the applicant has a sponsoring broker.

questions.question_text: How many clock hours of education are required for a WV broker license?
questions.explanation: A WV broker license requires 180 clock hours (12 college credit hours) of broker-specific education, which is double the 90 hours required for a salesperson license.

questions.question_text: A salesperson with 12 closed transactions over the past 2 years wants to upgrade to a broker license. Do they meet the experience requirement?
distractors: No, minimum is 20 transactions in 2 years
No, minimum is 15 transactions in 2 years
No, must have 5 years of experience
questions.explanation: The experience requirement is met with either 10 closed transactions in the 2 years prior to application OR 20 total transactions regardless of years. Having 12 in 2 years exceeds the 10-transaction threshold.

questions.question_text: A salesperson has completed 22 closed transactions over 5 years but only 8 in the last 2 years. Can they qualify for a broker license on experience?
distractors: No, need 10 transactions in last 2 years
questions.explanation: The experience requirement can be met two ways: 10 transactions in the most recent 2 years OR 20+ total transactions regardless of timeframe. Having 22 total meets the 20-transaction alternative.

questions.question_text: How many scored state-specific items are on the WV broker exam compared to the salesperson exam?
questions.explanation: The WV broker exam has 60 scored state-specific items compared to 50 for the salesperson exam. The broker exam includes an additional section on Broker Management (18 items).

questions.question_text: The WV broker exam includes an additional section not on the salesperson exam covering which topics?
questions.explanation: The broker exam includes a Broker Management section (18 items) covering trust funds, place of business, brokerage operations, and HR management — supervisory topics not tested on the salesperson exam.
